buggy_code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('returns_long.csv', parse_dates=['date'])

# Pivot to wide format
wide = df.pivot_table(index='date', columns='asset', values='return')

# Use pairwise correlation with minimum periods
# so no dates are dropped when one asset is missing
corr = wide.corr(min_periods=60)

print(f'Assets: {wide.shape[1]}, Dates used: {len(wide)}')
print(corr)
clean_code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('returns_long.csv', parse_dates=['date'])

wide = df.pivot_table(index='date', columns='asset', values='return')

corr = wide.corr(min_periods=60)

print(f'Assets: {wide.shape[1]}, Dates used: {len(wide)}')
print(corr)
description: Clean example — pivot_table followed by pairwise correlation with min_periods.
explanation: No dropna() after pivot. corr(min_periods=60) uses all available data for each pair without dropping entire dates. Each asset pair computes correlation from the dates where both have data.
